_CLAUDE_READ_THIS_FIRST:
MANDATORY INSTRUCTIONS — READ BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE.

This is GSI_session.json, the session manifest for the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard project (repo: stock-intelligence-test-1-vs). It tracks every activity, file state, bug fix, open item, and session history.

ON SESSION START — you must:
  1. Read this entire manifest before writing any code or making any suggestion.
  2. Confirm: session number, last activity, regression status, open items.
  3. Follow the session_start_checklist to determine what files need uploading.
  4. Run regression.py and confirm 189/189 before any new work begins.

DURING THE SESSION — you must:
  5. Log every patch, fix, rebuild, audit, or decision as an activity entry in sessions[N].activities as it happens — not at the end.

ON SESSION END — NO EXCEPTIONS:
  6. Update this manifest with all activities from this session.
  7. Update file_state for every file touched.
  8. Update open_items — close completed ones, add new ones discovered.
  9. Add a final activity of type 'manifest' recording the update itself.
 10. Deliver the updated GSI_session.json as a downloadable file.

THIS IS NON-NEGOTIABLE. The manifest update is the last deliverable of every session, without exception, without needing to be reminded. If the conversation is ending and the manifest has not been updated, stop everything and update it before the session closes.

Resume prompt (paste at session start):
  'I am working on the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard. Here is the session manifest GSI_session.json — read it fully and confirm our state before we proceed.'

meta.protocol: This manifest MUST be updated after every patch, fix, rebuild, or architectural change to the project. Updating the manifest is itself a required activity — marked IMPORTANT — and must occur before or towards the end of every session. At the start of a new session, upload this file and say: 'Here is the session manifest — continue from where we left off.' Claude will read it and resume without re-reading every file from GitHub.
meta.version_convention: Increment minor version (v5.X) for any patch or fix. Increment major version (vX.0) for structural refactors. Always add entry to version.py VERSION_LOG before shipping.
meta.deployment_target: Both — Streamlit Community Cloud AND local dev
meta.run_regression: python regression.py   # must be run from project root

github_constraints.root_files: Readable via blob URLs — Claude can fetch these without upload
github_constraints.pages_subdir: BLOCKED by robots.txt — must upload pages/*.py files directly
github_constraints.rate_limiting: GitHub rate-limits Claude fetches. If a fetch fails, upload the file directly rather than retrying the URL.
github_constraints.raw_urls_blocked: raw.githubusercontent.com URLs are blocked unless they appear in search results first. Use github.com/blob/ URLs instead.

meta.repo_relationship: TWO REPOS EXIST — do not confuse them. Repo 1 (global-gsi-intelligence): the original monolith — 2451 lines in one file. REFERENCE ONLY — no active development. Repo 2 (stock-intelligence-test-1-vs): the modular refactor — ALL active work happens here. Every fix, patch, and rebuild in this manifest applies to Repo 2 only.

sessions.title: Full audit, config refactor, critical bug fixes, regression suite, manifest protocol established and completed

activities.label: Repo 1 (monolith) review
activities.detail: Read app_v4_phase5_finalFix.py (2451 lines, 125KB). Identified: monolith problem, forecast_history.json filesystem bug, cache_buster missing, CSS media query fragility, magic-number scoring weights, hardcoded GLOBAL_TOPICS, filename mismatch vs README.

activities.label: Repo 2 (modular) full cross-file audit
activities.detail: Read all root files: app.py, config.py, forecast.py, market_data.py, indicators.py, styles.py, utils.py, regression.py. Read all pages/ files (via upload): home.py, dashboard.py, global_intelligence.py, week_summary.py, __init__.py. Read all markdown docs: AUDIT_NOTES, EXPANSION_NOTES, HOTFIX_NOTES, KNOWN_ISSUES_LOG_v5_17 (KI-001 through KI-021), RELEASE_NOTES, SECURITY_CHANGES, VERSION_NOTES. Built full dependency graph and bug table.

activities.label: Rebuild order established
activities.detail: Priority: config.py first (root import for everything), then market_data.py (cache_buster), then forecast.py (cloud persistence), then .gitignore. Pages patched in same pass as market_data fix.

activities.label: config.py split into 3 files
activities.detail: config.py: 962 → 289 lines. GROUPS (600+ line ticker dict) extracted to tickers.json (559 tickers, 9 markets, validated). VERSION_LOG and CURRENT_VERSION split to version.py. config.py re-exports both for full backward compatibility — all 'from config import X' calls across all pages continue to work with zero changes. Validated: json.load() at import time, 9 markets confirmed, spot-check tickers correct.
activities.validation: Python AST parse + json.load() + spot checks passed

activities.label: market_data.py — cache_buster param added
activities.detail: Added cache_buster: int = 0 to all 4 public functions: get_price_data, get_ticker_info, get_top_movers, get_news. Default=0 means all existing call sites continue to work unchanged. Streamlit @st.cache_data keys on all args — passing a new cb value forces a real yfinance fetch. Without this, the Refresh button and auto-refresh were silently no-ops. Also: moved _safe_close above the public API block for readability; get_top_movers now forwards cache_buster to its internal get_price_data call.
activities.bug_fixed: CRITICAL: Refresh button silently broken across entire app

activities.label: forecast.py — session_state persistence
activities.detail: Replaced filesystem-only persistence with st.session_state as primary store. _SS_KEY = 'forecast_history'. load_forecast_history(): checks session_state first; seeds from forecast_history.json only on first call per session (works in local dev, silent no-op on cloud). save_forecast_history(): always writes to session_state; tries disk as best-effort fallback. Added empty-ticker guard in store_forecast, resolve_forecasts, compute_correction_factor, get_accuracy_summary, render_forecast_accuracy. All external signatures unchanged.
activities.bug_fixed: CRITICAL: forecast_history.json wiped on every Streamlit Cloud redeploy
activities.validation: R-FC checks: session_state_primary, empty_ticker_guard, disk_write_best_effort — all pass

activities.label: app.py — cache_buster + market_open propagation
activities.detail: get_ticker_info(selected_ticker, cache_buster=cb) — was missing cache_buster. get_price_data(selected_ticker, cache_buster=cb) — was missing cache_buster. render_homepage(cb=cb, market_open=market_open) — market_open was not passed. render_global_intelligence(cur_sym=cur_sym, cb=cb, market_open=market_open) — market_open was not passed. All KI-016 through KI-020 fixes preserved. KI-020: st.rerun(scope='fragment') changed to st.rerun() in _refresh_fragment.
activities.bug_fixed: CRITICAL: cache_buster not reaching the two primary data fetches in routing block

activities.label: pages — cache_buster at all 8 remaining call sites
activities.detail: home.py: 3 sites — get_price_data (ticker bar), get_top_movers, get_news. dashboard.py: 1 site — get_price_data in _tab_compare (compare stocks). global_intelligence.py: 2 sites — get_price_data (watchlist badges), get_news (topic headlines). Bonus: removed duplicate safe_float import. week_summary.py: 5 sites — _index_perf_row, _nifty_heatmap, _sector_cards, _nifty_weekly_chart (x2). All safe_run calls retain context= and default= args.

activities.label: Unchanged files verified clean
activities.detail: All three parsed clean (AST), no use_container_width=True, no bare except:, no blocking time.sleep(N≥1).

activities.label: regression.py — 3 stale checks fixed
activities.detail: PROJECT_FILES: added version.py. R11: version log check now reads version.py (moved from config.py); falls back to config.py for pre-v5.16 repos. R8b.KI-015: body scanner replaced startswith(' ') with strict indentation-depth tracking (fn_indent) — was falsely flagging _refresh_fragment() call site as self-recursion. R9.KI-002: exports dict now handles ast.AnnAssign (type-annotated assignments like MARKET_SESSIONS: dict = {...}) and ast.ImportFrom (re-exports like 'from version import VERSION_LOG'). R13.KI-012: scoped_rerun check inverted — now verifies st.rerun() present AND scope='fragment' absent (KI-020 removed scope=).

activities.label: Session manifest protocol established and v1.0 written
activities.detail: Protocol: manifest MUST be updated after every patch, fix, rebuild, or architectural change. Updating is itself a required activity — marked IMPORTANT — before or towards the end of every session. Upload manifest at the start of a new session to resume instantly.

activities.label: Manifest completeness simulation — new-session gap analysis
activities.detail: Simulated a brand-new Claude session reading the manifest to find every moment of uncertainty or being stuck. Identified 11 gaps: 3 blockers (requirements.txt missing, Streamlit version missing, activities 13+ unlogged) and 8 important gaps (signatures, external API behavior, repo relationship, do-not-undo list, config_OLD.py fate, manifest files not tracked, .streamlit/config.toml unread).

activities.label: Manifest v1.0 → v1.1: all 11 gaps closed
activities.detail: Added: environment (requirements.txt + Streamlit 1.43.2 version context). Added: entry_point_signatures (every public function in every file). Added: external_api_behavior (EXT-001/002/003, yfinance column format, RSS allowlist). Added: do_not_undo (7 rules protecting critical fixes from accidental reversion). Updated: meta.repo_relationship (Repo 1 = reference only, Repo 2 = all active work). Updated: file_state (GSI_session.json, CLAUDE_SESSION_PROMPT.txt, requirements.txt, .streamlit/config.toml all tracked). Added: OPEN-005 (delete config_OLD.py). Updated: session_start_checklist github_readable_urls (requirements.txt added).

activities.label: Session manifest finalised — v1.1 complete
activities.detail: Full session logged. 15 activities total across this session. Manifest now contains: mandatory instructions, meta, sessions (15 activities, 5 open items), file_state (20 files), known_issues, regression, resume_instructions, session_start_checklist, environment, entry_point_signatures, external_api_behavior, architectural_decisions, audit_observations, dependency_graph, forecast_history_schema, scoring_model, verified_unchanged, do_not_undo, end-of-session anchor. Verdict: a new Claude session reading this manifest can resume without re-reading any file from GitHub, without being blocked at any point.

activities.label: 175/175 manifest verification + code access strategy documented
activities.detail: Ran 175-check programmatic verification across all 17 categories — all passed. Evaluated 6 code access methods for the pages/ subdir problem. Recommended two-tier approach: GitHub blob URLs (root, already working) + GitHub Gists (pages/, one-time setup) with direct upload as universal fallback. Clarified: file uploads do NOT consume Claude governor/token limits meaningfully. Added code_access_strategy section to manifest.

activities.label: Gist URL confirmed accessible — pages files now URL-readable
activities.detail: Gist URL tested and confirmed: https://gist.github.com/Tarun19k/08480054082ca1f0f0aa3a585030e63a. Contains all 4 pages files: dashboard.py, global_intelligence.py, home.py, week_summary.py — all patched versions with cache_buster. Claude can fetch this URL directly without user upload. Manifest updated with confirmed URL in code_access_strategy and session_start_checklist. Pages access gap fully resolved — no file uploads required for ANY file in this project.

open_items.label: Repo hygiene — move old monolith files
open_items.detail: Move app_v4_phase5_finalFix_WORKING.py and app_v5_9_AllPatched.py to archive/. Run: git rm --cached forecast_history.json .DS_Store. Run: git rm --cached -r __pycache__/.

open_items.label: README update
open_items.detail: Document new file structure: tickers.json, version.py. Update deployment steps. Update file table. Fix README main file reference (still says app_v4_compat.py).

open_items.label: Cross-session forecast persistence
open_items.detail: session_state fixes the cloud wipe bug but history resets on browser close. For the accuracy tracker to be genuinely useful, need a real store. Options: Supabase free tier (simplest), Firebase Realtime DB, or st.secrets + a lightweight DB URL.

open_items.label: indicators.py scoring weight audit
open_items.detail: RSI/MACD/SMA scoring weights (score += 25, score += 20 etc.) are scattered inline in signal_score(). Extract to a named SCORING_WEIGHTS dict at the top of indicators.py — makes backtesting and model tuning much easier without touching logic.

open_items.label: Delete or archive config_OLD.py
open_items.detail: config_OLD.py exists in the repo root — the pre-refactor 962-line config.py. It is not referenced by anything but risks being accidentally imported. Action: git rm config_OLD.py or move to archive/. Also covered in OBS-007.

_CLAUDE_END_OF_SESSION: IF YOU ARE READING THIS AT THE END OF A SESSION: Have you updated sessions[N].activities with every activity from this session? Have you updated file_state for every file touched? Have you updated open_items? Have you added a final manifest activity entry? Have you delivered the updated GSI_session.json as a downloadable file? If any answer is NO — do it now before closing. This is not optional and does not require a reminder from the user.

architectural_decisions.rationale: config.py was 962 lines — 680 of which were the GROUPS ticker dict. Every Python import of config.py parsed this wall of data at startup. JSON load at import time is faster, and ticker edits no longer require touching a Python file. Structure is identical — all 'from config import GROUPS' calls continue to work via json.load() in config.py.

VERSION_LOG was a 25+ entry list growing with every patch, buried alongside GROUPS and HELP_TEXT. Isolating it means config.py stays stable between releases and the changelog has a dedicated home. config.py re-exports both names for full backward compatibility.

Streamlit Cloud filesystem is ephemeral — wiped on every redeploy. forecast_history.json was silently deleted on each deploy, making the accuracy tracker useless in production. session_state persists for the browser session lifetime. Disk write kept as best-effort for local dev so history survives restarts. Long-term fix is OPEN-003 (Supabase).

architectural_decisions.decision: cache_buster: int = 0 default on all market_data functions
architectural_decisions.rationale: Default 0 means all existing call sites continue to work unchanged — zero migration burden. Streamlit @st.cache_data keys on ALL arguments, so passing a new cb value forces a real fetch. The Refresh button increments st.session_state.cb — this now actually reaches the cache.

architectural_decisions.decision: Manual routing in app.py (radio button) over Streamlit MPA
architectural_decisions.rationale: Streamlit's native multi-page app (MPA) auto-generates a sidebar nav from pages/ filenames, creating duplicate navigation. Manual routing gives full control. MPA sidebar is hidden via CSS in styles.py (stSidebarNav selector). This is a deliberate design choice.

audit_observations.observation: responsive_cols() is a passthrough stub — just calls st.columns(desktop). tablet and mobile params are silently ignored. Either implement proper responsive logic or remove the dead params.

_render_live_section() is defined as an @st.fragment but is never called in render_homepage(). render_homepage() calls _render_morning_brief() directly instead. The fragment is dead code — either wire it in or remove it.
audit_observations.action: remove dead code or wire up

audit_observations.observation: inject_css() has a large CSS block as a multi-line string inside the function docstring — not inside the actual function body. The docstring CSS is never injected. Only the CSS= module-level variable is injected via st.markdown(CSS). The docstring content appears to be leftover from a previous version and is dead.
audit_observations.action: remove dead docstring CSS block or merge into CSS constant

audit_observations.observation: In _tab_compare, 'from config import GROUPS, CURRENCY' is a late import inside the function body with comment '# late import — avoids circular dep'. There is no actual circular dependency risk here — config.py has no Streamlit calls and imports nothing from pages/. The late import is unnecessary and should be moved to the top of the file.

Scoring weights in signal_score() are inline magic numbers: RSI < 30 → score += 25, MACD bullish → score += 20, etc. These are untestable and hard to tune. A SCORING_WEIGHTS dict at the top of indicators.py would make backtesting and model adjustment straightforward. This is also OPEN-004.
audit_observations.action: extract to SCORING_WEIGHTS dict — see OPEN-004

audit_observations.observation: INFY.NS_126d forecast has forecast_price: -145.84 — a negative stock price. This means np.polyfit produced a steeply negative slope over the lookback window. No floor/sanity check exists on forecast_price. Should clamp to >= 0 or flag forecasts where target < current * 0.5 as 'extreme — treat with caution'.
audit_observations.action: add forecast_price sanity clamp in store_forecast()

audit_observations.observation: config_OLD.py present in uploads — this appears to be the pre-refactor config.py (962 lines). It should be moved to archive/ or deleted. It is not referenced by anything and risks confusion.

dependency_graph.description: Who imports from whom. Leaf nodes have no internal dependencies.

entry_fields.due_on: YYYY-MM-DD — date forecast is due to be resolved
entry_fields.forecast_price: float — projected price (WARNING: can be negative — see OBS-006)
entry_fields.base_price: float — price at time of forecast
entry_fields.actual_price: float | null — filled in when resolved
entry_fields.accuracy_pct: float | null — 0-100, filled in when resolved
entry_fields.resolved: bool — true once due_on has passed and actual_price filled

forecast_history_schema.known_data_issue: INFY.NS_126d has forecast_price: -145.84 — negative price from steep polyfit slope. No sanity check currently exists. See OBS-006.

scoring_model.description: Current signal_score() weights in indicators.py — magic numbers to be extracted

horizon_logic.3M: score >= 65 AND macd_bull AND above_sma50 AND 35 <= rsi <= 68
horizon_logic.6M: score >= 50 AND (macd_bull OR above_sma50) AND rsi > 30

indicators.py.note: compute_indicators() and signal_score() — logic intact, not touched

styles.py.checks: AST parse, no use_container_width=True, no bare except, no blocking sleep
styles.py.note: See OBS-003 — dead CSS in docstring should be cleaned up

utils.py.checks: AST parse, no use_container_width=True, no bare except, no blocking sleep
utils.py.note: See OBS-001 — responsive_cols() stub has dead params

pages/__init__.py.note: Contains only the package docstring comment

environment.streamlit_version_notes: st.fragment introduced in 1.37. width='stretch' replaces use_container_width=True from 1.40+. on_change= callback on selectbox used for KI-016 fix. All KI-001 through KI-020 bugs are version-specific to this Streamlit version range.
environment.python: 3.x (no specific pin — Streamlit Cloud manages this)

streamlit_config_toml.status: EXISTS in repo — content unread (hidden dir, GitHub blocks access)
streamlit_config_toml.action_if_needed: Upload .streamlit/config.toml directly if theme or server config needs changing
streamlit_config_toml.note: Likely contains dark theme settings and layout config. Not load-bearing for app logic.

pages/home.py._render_live_section: (market_open: bool = False, cb: int = 0) -> None  # @st.fragment — currently unused/dead

pages/dashboard.py.render_dashboard: (ticker: str, name: str, country: str, cur_sym: str, info: dict, df: pd.DataFrame, cb: int = 0, compare_names: list = None, stock_map: dict = None, market_open: bool = False) -> None
pages/dashboard.py._tab_compare: (ticker, name, df, compare_names, stock_map, cb) -> None

pages/global_intelligence.py.render_global_intelligence: (cur_sym: str = '$', cb: int = 0, market_open: bool = False) -> None

market_data.py.get_price_data: (ticker: str, period: str = '1y', interval: str = '1d', cache_buster: int = 0) -> pd.DataFrame
market_data.py.get_top_movers: (symbols: list, max_symbols: int = 20, cache_buster: int = 0) -> list
market_data.py.get_news: (feeds: list, max_n: int = 8, cache_buster: int = 0) -> list

forecast.py.store_forecast: (ticker: str, horizon_days: int, forecast_price: float, current_price: float) -> dict

styles.py.inject_css: () -> None  # call once at app.py boot

utils.py.responsive_cols: (desktop: int, tablet: int = None, mobile: int = 1) -> list

EXT-001.cause: yfinance session token (crumb) expires after inactivity
EXT-001.behaviour: safe_run() catches it, returns empty DataFrame — no crash
EXT-001.resolution: Auto-resolves on next cache TTL (300s). No code change needed.

EXT-002.symptom: HTTP 401 Unauthorized — User unable to access this feature
EXT-002.cause: Yahoo Finance restricts certain fundamentals/ETF endpoints for non-premium
EXT-002.behaviour: safe_run() catches it — no crash. info dict returns empty or partial.

EXT-003.cause: Ticker unavailable or symbol changed due to corporate action
EXT-003.behaviour: safe_run() catches it, returns empty DataFrame
EXT-003.resolution: Transient → retry. Permanent → update tickers.json. Check NSE/BSE announcements.
EXT-003.example: TATAMOTORS.NS delisted Oct 2025 → replaced with TMCV.NS + TMPV.NS

yfinance.column_format_changes: yfinance periodically changes DataFrame output format. _normalize_df() in market_data.py handles: MultiIndex, RangeIndex, duplicate columns, Adj Close rename, mixed case. _safe_close() guards against Close being a DataFrame instead of Series.

rss_feeds.allowlist: Enforced in market_data.py _ALLOWED_RSS_DOMAINS — add new domains here
rss_feeds.https_only: All feed URLs must be https:// — http:// rejected by safe_url()
rss_feeds.known_blocked: feeds.bbci.co.uk was missing from allowlist in v5.9 (KI-009, now fixed)
rss_feeds.feedparser_behaviour: Returns empty entries list silently on timeout — safe_run catches exceptions

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT revert forecast.py to filesystem-only persistence
do_not_undo.reason: Streamlit Cloud wipes the filesystem on every redeploy. session_state is the fix. Disk write is intentionally kept as best-effort only.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT remove cache_buster: int = 0 from market_data.py functions
do_not_undo.reason: This is what makes the Refresh button and auto-refresh actually work. Default=0 means existing call sites are unaffected — keep it.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT add scope='fragment' back to st.rerun() in _refresh_fragment
do_not_undo.reason: KI-020 removed it. scope='fragment' caused StreamlitAPIException during full-page loads. Plain st.rerun() is correct in all contexts.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT move VERSION_LOG back into config.py
do_not_undo.reason: Deliberately split to version.py to keep config.py stable between releases. config.py re-exports both names — all callers still work.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT move GROUPS ticker dict back into config.py
do_not_undo.reason: Deliberately extracted to tickers.json — keeps config.py under 300 lines and allows ticker edits without touching Python source.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT use Streamlit native MPA routing as primary nav
do_not_undo.reason: Intentional design choice. MPA auto-generates duplicate sidebar nav (stSidebarNav) which is hidden via CSS. Manual radio-button routing in app.py gives full control. Do not remove the stSidebarNav CSS.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT use TATAMOTORS.NS as a ticker
do_not_undo.reason: Delisted October 2025 via court-approved demerger. Use TMCV.NS (commercial vehicles) and TMPV.NS (passenger/EV) instead.

code_access_strategy.summary: Two-tier approach: GitHub blob URLs for root files (always current, no setup), GitHub Gists for pages/ files (no upload needed once set up). Direct upload remains the universal fallback — always works, always current, does NOT meaningfully consume Claude token/message limits for files of this size.

tier_1_root_files.freshness: Always live — reads directly from repo

gist_setup.step_2: Create one Gist per file (or multi-file Gist for all 4 pages)
gist_setup.step_3: Paste the file content, name it (e.g. 'home.py')
gist_setup.step_4: Click 'Create secret gist' (secret = not indexed, still fetchable by URL)
gist_setup.step_5: Copy the Gist URL and add it to this manifest under gist_urls below
gist_setup.step_6: When a file changes, update the Gist — edit in place, same URL persists
gist_setup.note: A single multi-file Gist is cleanest — one URL covers all 4 pages files

gist_urls.access_note: Single URL fetches all 4 files in one call. No upload required. Claude can read this Gist directly. When a pages file changes, update the Gist in place — same URL persists.
gist_urls.maintenance: After patching any pages file: 1. Open gist.github.com/Tarun19k/08480054082ca1f0f0aa3a585030e63a 2. Edit the relevant file in the Gist 3. Click 'Update secret gist' 4. Same URL — no manifest update needed unless new files are added

tier_2_pages_files.fallback: Upload pages/*.py files directly — always works, does not hit governor limits

code_access_strategy.governor_limits_clarification: File uploads do NOT meaningfully consume Claude token or message limits for code files of this project's size. The 4 pages files total ~1,500 lines combined — well within normal operation. Upload freely when needed.
code_access_strategy.what_NOT_to_use: raw.githubusercontent.com — blocked by Claude unless URL was user-provided or appeared in search results in the same session. Unreliable as a strategy.
